content:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : High density polythene is formed in the presence of catalyst triethylaluminium and titanium tetrachloride.

Statement II : High density polymers are chemically inert.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :

options.content: Statement I is correct but Statement II is false.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are false.

explanation: NCERT Pg. 436 (Polymer)

content: Which amongst the following molecules on polymerization produces neoprene?
Which among the following is a thermoplastic polymer?
explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Polythene is an example of thermoplastic polymer.

Melamine polymer, Bakelite and Urea-formaldehyde resin are thermosetting polymers.

content: Which statement regarding polymers is not correct?

options.content: Elastomers have polymer chains held together by weak intermolecular forces
Thermoplastic polymers are capable of repeatedly softening and hardening on heating and cooling respectively

explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Thermoplastic polymers are the linear or slightly long chain molecules capable of repeatedly softening and hardening on cooling.

$$\bullet$$ Thermosetting polymers are cross-linked or heavily branched molecules, which on heating undergo extensive cross-linking in moulds and again become infusible. These cannot be reused.

$$\bullet$$ Elastomers have polymer chains held together by weak intermolecular forces.

$$\bullet$$ Fibres possess high tensile strength.

content: Which one of the following polymers is prepared by addition polymerisation?
explanation:
Dacron, Nylon-66 and Novolac are prepared by condensation polymerisation.

Teflon is an addition polymer. Monomer of teflon is tetrafluoroethene.

content: The reagent 'R' in the given sequence of chemical reaction is :
explanation: Reagent R is C2H5OH with diazonium salt.

content: Which of the following is a natural polymer?
explanation: Naturally occurring polymer, natural rubber is cis-1, 4-polyisoprene.

content: The biodegradable polymer is :
explanation: Biodegradable polymer is Nylon-2-Nylon-6.

content:
Regarding cross-linked or network polymers,
which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.content:
They contain covalent bonds between
various linear polymer chains.

They are formed from bi- and tri-functional
monomers.

They contain strong covalent bonds in
their polymer chains.

explanation:
Cross-linked or network polymers are usually
formed from bi-functional & tri-functional
monomers and contains strong covalent bond
between various linear polymer chains like
Melamine, Bakelite etc.

explanation: Thermosetting polymers undergo chemical changes when heated and set to hard mass when cooled e.g. Bakelite.

content: Which of the following organic compounds polymerizes to form the polyester dacron?
Nylon is an example of
explanation: Nylon is a polyamide.

content: Which is the monomer of neoprene in the following?
explanation: Chloroprene is the monomer of neoprene.

content: Which one of the following sets forms the biodegradable polymer?
explanation: Biodegradable polymer is Nylon-2-Nylon-6 which is copolymer of glycine (H<sub>2</sub>N – CH<sub>2</sub>– COOH) and amino caproic acid (H<sub>2</sub>N–(CH<sub>2</sub>)<sub>5</sub> – COOH).

content: Which one of the following is not a condensation polymer?
explanation:
Neoprene is made by joining smaller molecules together without losing any atoms. This process is called addition polymerization.

Condensation polymers are made when small molecules, like water, are released during the reaction. Neoprene does not form this way.

So, Neoprene is not a condensation polymer.

content: Which of the following statements is false?

options.content: Artificial silk is derived from cellulose.
Nylon-6, 6 is an example of elastomer.
The repeat unit in natural rubber is isoprene.
Both starch and cellulose are polymers of glucose.

explanation: Nylon 6, 6 is an example of fibers.

content: Of the following which one is classified as polyester polymer?
explanation: Terylene (Dacron) is a polyester polymer.

content: Which of the following structures represents neoprene polymer?
explanation:
Neoprene is :


It is a polymer of chloroprene.

content: Which one of the following statements is not true?

options.content: Buna-S is a copolymer of butadiene and styrene.
Natural rubber is a, 1,4-polymer of isoprene.
In vulcanization, the formation of sulphur bridges between different chains make rubber harder and stronger.
Natural rubber has the trans-configuration at every double bond.

explanation: Natural rubber is cis-1, 3 polyisoprene and has only cis-configuration about the double bond.

content: Which  one of the following polymers is prepared by condensation polymerisation?
explanation: Nylon-6,6 is a condensation polymer of adipic acid and hexamethylene diamine.

explanation: It is formed by the condensation of adipic acid and hexamethylene diamine. It is a copolymer.

Which one of the following is a chain growth polymer?
explanation: Polystyrene is a chain growth polymer.

content: Acrilan is a hard, horny and a high melting material. Which one of the following represents its structure?
explanation: Acrilan is an addition polymer of acrylonitrile.

content: Which one of the following monomers gives the polymer neoprene on polymerization?
explanation: Chloroprene (2-chloro-1, 3-butadiene) on addition polymerisation gives neoprene.

content: CF<sub>2</sub> $$=$$ CF<sub>2</sub> is monomer of
Which compound forms linear polymer due to H-bond?
explanation:
HF form linear polymer due to H-bonding.

H - F .... H - F .... H - F .... H - F

Due to high electronegativity value of 'F' atom it forms effective hydrogen bonds.
